standard: CEQ NEPA and Permitting Data and Technology Standard v1.2 (August 18, 2025)
mfr: MFR 4 — Decision-logic export for CE screening rules
source: NEPA_CE_Screening_Rule__mdt — 71 active rules derived from NEPATEC v2.0 corpus (61,881 NEPA project records, PNNL 2025)
description: Categorical Exclusion screening rules used by the NEPA_CE_Screener BRE Expression Set to classify NEPA applications into CE, EA, or EIS review pathways. Rules are organized in two tiers: Tier 1 are direct agency+sector+type rules calibrated from the NEPATEC corpus; Tier 2 are override or fallback rules that handle extraordinary-circumstances flags, adjacent statute triggers, and cross-agency defaults.

tier_1.description: Direct match rules: agency + sector + project type → CE code + review type

rules.regulatory_citation: 42 U.S.C. § 15942(b)(3) — oil and gas exploration and production activities on previously disturbed lands
disqualifiers: New surface disturbance exceeding acreage threshold
Located in designated Wilderness or Wilderness Study Area
ESA Critical Habitat intersects project footprint

rules.regulatory_citation: 42 U.S.C. § 15942(b)(3) — transmission line activities on previously disturbed lands
disqualifiers: Crosses navigable waters requiring Section 404 permit

rules.regulatory_citation: 516 DM 11.9 — routine management actions with no or minimal disturbance
10 C.F.R. Part 1021 Subpart D, B3.6 — small-scale R&D activities
disqualifiers: Generates hazardous materials requiring RCRA permit

rules.regulatory_citation: 10 C.F.R. Part 1021 Subpart D, B1.3 — operation and maintenance of existing facilities
10 C.F.R. Part 1021 Subpart D, B1.3 — operation and maintenance of existing facilities
disqualifiers: Involves Section 404/401 waters of the US

rules.regulatory_citation: 10 C.F.R. Part 1021 Subpart D, B1.25 — site characterization studies
disqualifiers: Area includes listed species critical habitat
Involves ground disturbance in riparian zone

rules.regulatory_citation: 36 C.F.R. § 220.6(e)(15) — reissuance of existing special use permits with no or minor changes
disqualifiers: Renewal includes substantial modification of terms
Original permit was issued without NEPA review

tier_2.description: Override and fallback rules applied after Tier 1: extraordinary-circumstances flags, adjacent statute triggers, cross-agency defaults
tier_2.evaluation_order: Evaluated in order after Tier 1 match; override wins if condition met

rules.trigger_condition: nepa_extraordinary_circumstances__c = true AND (ESA Critical Habitat OR Tribal Sacred Sites flag set)
rules.description: Any project triggering ESA Critical Habitat or Tribal Sacred Sites extraordinary circumstances requires minimum EA regardless of Tier 1 CE recommendation
rules.regulatory_basis: 40 C.F.R. § 1501.4(b) — extraordinary circumstances preclude CE use; CEQ 2022 regulations

rules.description: Well abandonment and decommissioning on BLM lands qualifies for CE under DOI regulations when disturbance is limited to plugging and reclamation activities
DOE demolition and disposal activities at existing facilities qualify for B2.5 CE when no hazardous materials are involved beyond de minimis levels
rules.regulatory_citation: 10 C.F.R. Part 1021 Subpart D, B2.5 — demolition and disposal

extraordinary_circumstances_factors: ESA-listed species or designated critical habitat in project area
Tribal sacred sites, tribal lands, or Section 106 NHPA resources
Wildlands, wilderness study areas, roadless areas
Areas with significant cumulative NEPA activity within 5 years
GIS proximity flag: USGS NHD waterway within buffer
GIS proximity flag: BLM tribal boundary intersection
GIS proximity flag: BLM PLSS public land survey intersection

scoring_integration.description: CE screening confidence scores are inputs to the Litigation Risk Scorer (see litigation-risk-weights.json). A CE recommendation with Low confidence adds a risk penalty; an EA or EIS recommendation with any confirmed extraordinary circumstance adds the challenge prediction rule penalty.
